title: desmos ramarowii
intro: Desmos ramarowii, also known as the Indian boxwood or Indian cherry, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of India, particularly in the Western Ghats. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Desmos ramarowii include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. Desmos ramarowii can be prepared as a decoction or a paste, and its leaves and bark are used in various herbal preparations, including teas and topical creams.
